memberdef.argsstring: (GGC &C, GGA &A, GGA &Acc, GGB &B, GGB &Bcc)
memberdef.name: Bubble1

para.bold: Evaluate a bubble diagram ( $C$) from two-time contour functions $A,B$ at the time step; $ C_{0,0}(t_1,t_2) = i A_{0,0}(t_1,t_2) * B_{0,0}(t_2,t_1) $.

detaileddescription.para:
Purpose
Evaluate the two-time contour function $C$ represented as a bubble diagram with two-time functions $A,B$; $ C_{0,0}(t_1,t_2) = i A_{0,0}(t_1,t_2) * B_{0,0}(t_2,t_1) $. Here it is assmued that $A$, $B$ and $C$ are $1\times 1$ matrices. This evaluation is done at the time step (i.e. $ t_1 $ or $ t_2 $ is the time step) for all components (retarded, lesser, left-mixing and Matsubara). The evaluated value of $ i A_{0,0}(t_1,t_2) * B_{0,0}(t_2,t_1)$ is stored at $ C_{0,0}(t_1,t_2)$.

parameterdescription.para: Two-time contour object in the $1\times 1$-Matrix form defined as $ C_{0,0}(t_1,t_2) = i A_{0,0}(t_1,t_2) * B_{0,0}(t_2,t_1) $.
Two-time contour object in the $1\times 1$-Matrix form.
Two-time contour object in the $1\times 1$-Matrix form, which is adjoint of $A$.
Two-time contour object in the $1\times 1$-Matrix form.
Two-time contour object in the $1\times 1$-Matrix form, which is adjoint of $B$.

This evaluation is done at the time step (i.e. $ t_1 $ or $ t_2 $ is the time step) for all components (retarded, lesser, left-mixing and Matsubara). The evaluated value of $ i A_{0,0}(t_1,t_2) * B_{0,0}(t_2,t_1)$ is stored at $ C_{0,0}(t_1,t_2)$. Here $A$ and $B$ are assumed to have the hermitian symmetry.

para.bold: One step Dyson solver (integral-differential form) for a Green's function $G$

blockquote.para: One solves the Dyson equation of the following form: $ [ id/dt + \mu - H(t) ] G(t,t^\prime) - [\Sigma*G](t,t^\prime) = \delta(t,t^\prime)$ for a hermitian matrix $G(t, t^\prime)$ on a truncated time window, i.e., $ G^{\mathrm{R}}(nh,t'<=nh) $, $ G^<(nh,t'<=nh)$ . Timestep must be $ >=t_c>=k$ , where tc is the cutoff time and k is the integration order . Here, are given: $\Sigma(t, t^\prime)$, $\mu$, and $H(t)$.

memberdef.argsstring: (herm_matrix_moving< T > &G, herm_matrix_moving< T > &Sigma, function_moving< T > &eps, T mu, integration::Integrator< T > &I, T h)
memberdef.name: dyson_timestep

para.bold: One step Dyson solver (integral-differential form) for a Green's function $G$

blockquote.para: One solves the Dyson equation of the following form: $ [ id/dt + \mu - H(t) ] G(t,t^\prime) - [\Sigma*G](t,t^\prime) = \delta(t,t^\prime)$ for a hermitian matrix $G(t, t^\prime)$ on a truncated time window, i.e., $ G^{\mathrm{R}}(nh,t'<=nh) $, $ G^<(nh,t'<=nh)$ . Timestep must be $ >=t_c>=k$ , where tc is the cutoff time and k is the integration order of 'I'. Here, are given: $\Sigma(t, t^\prime)$, $\mu$, and $H(t)$.

memberdef.argsstring: (int n, herm_matrix< T > &G, T mu, function< T > &H, herm_matrix< T > &Sigma, integration::Integrator< T > &I, T beta, T h)
memberdef.name: dyson_timestep

memberdef.name: dyson_timestep_omp

para.bold: One step Dyson solver (integral-differential form) for a Green's function $G$ using openMP parallelization

blockquote.para: One solves the Dyson equation of the following form: $ [ id/dt + \mu - H(t) ] G(t,t^\prime) - [\Sigma*G](t,t^\prime) = \delta(t,t^\prime)$ for a hermitian matrix $G(t, t^\prime)$ at a given timestep 'n', i.e., G^ret(nh,t'<=nh), G^les(t<=nh,nh), G^tv(nt,tau=0..beta). Timestep must be >k, where k is the Integration order 'I'. The timesteps n=0..k must be computed seperately, using the routine "_start", which assumes that the Matsubara component of $G$ and $\Sigma(t,t^\prime)$ for $t,t^\prime$<=k are given. Here, are given: $\Sigma(t, t^\prime)$, $\mu$, and $H(t)$.
[int] The number of openMP threads to be used. Set to the number of threads in the current team. If omp_num_threads = -1, the maximum available number of threads is used.

memberdef.argsstring: (int omp_num_threads, int n, GG &G, T mu, std::complex< T > *H, GG &Sigma, integration::Integrator< T > &I, T h)
memberdef.name: dyson_timestep_ret_omp

memberdef.argsstring: (int omp_num_threads, int n, GG &G, T mu, std::complex< T > *Hn, GG &Sigma, integration::Integrator< T > &I, T beta, T h)

memberdef.name: response_convolution

para.bold: Evaluate the convolution between a two-time contour function ( $W$) and a real-time function ( $f$) at the time step ( $t$); $ c(t) = \int_C dt' W_{a1,a2}(t,t') f_{b1,b2}(t') $.

detaileddescription.para:
Purpose
This function evaluates the convolution between a two-time contour function ( $W$) and a real-time function ( $f$) at the time step ( $t$); $ c(t) = \int_C dt' W_{a1,a2}(t,t') f_{b1,b2}(t') $. It can be used, for example, to evaluate a lineaer response by using a response function as $W$ and an external field as $f$.

parameterdescription.para: Value of the conovlution at the time step; $ c(t) = \int_C dt' W_{a1,a2}(t,t') f_{b1,b2}(t') $.
Two-time contour object in the Matrix form with the hermitian symmetry.
Function in the Matrix from on the real-time axis.

memberdef.name: response_convolution

para.bold: Evaluate the convolution between a two-time contour function ( $W$) and a real-time function ( $f$) at the time step ( $t$); $ c(t) = \int_C dt' W_{a1,a2}(t,t') f_{b1,b2}(t') $.

detaileddescription.para:

It can be used, for example, to evaluate a lineaer response by using a response function as $W$ and an external field as $f$.
